questions.q: Raja Ram Mohan Roy is widely called the father of which movement?
questions.explanation: He is honoured as the Father of the Indian Renaissance for his pioneering reforms.

questions.q: Which reform organization did Raja Ram Mohan Roy found in 1828?
questions.explanation: He founded the Brahmo Sabha in 1828, later known as the Brahmo Samaj.

questions.q: Raja Ram Mohan Roy campaigned famously against which social practice?
questions.explanation: He led the campaign against sati, the burning of widows, which was abolished in 1829.

questions.q: In which year did Raja Ram Mohan Roy die?
questions.explanation: Raja Ram Mohan Roy died in 1833 while in England.

questions.q: Raja Ram Mohan Roy strongly advocated for which kind of education in India?
questions.explanation: He championed modern, Western-style scientific education alongside reform.

questions.q: Raja Ram Mohan Roy worked chiefly in which region of India?
questions.explanation: He was a Bengali reformer based largely in Bengal and Calcutta.

questions.q: Raja Ram Mohan Roy also campaigned for the rights of whom?
questions.explanation: He campaigned for women's rights, including against sati and for inheritance and remarriage.

questions.q: The title 'Raja' was conferred on Ram Mohan Roy by which authority?
questions.explanation: The Mughal Emperor Akbar II conferred the title 'Raja' on Ram Mohan Roy.

questions.q: Raja Ram Mohan Roy's reformist legacy is described as shaping the intellectual ground of what?
questions.explanation: His reformism is seen as preparing the intellectual ground for the later freedom movement.

questions.q: In which country did Raja Ram Mohan Roy die during his travels?
questions.explanation: He died at Bristol, England, in 1833.

questions.q: Raja Ram Mohan Roy was born in 1772 in a village in which district of Bengal?
questions.explanation: He was born at Radhanagar in the Hooghly district of Bengal in 1772.

questions.q: The British Governor-General who abolished sati in 1829, supported by Roy's campaign, was who?
questions.explanation: Lord William Bentinck legally abolished sati in 1829 with Roy's strong backing.

questions.q: Raja Ram Mohan Roy published a Persian-language journal titled what?
questions.explanation: He published 'Mirat-ul-Akhbar,' a Persian newspaper, as part of his reform efforts.

questions.q: Roy's monotheistic outlook was reflected in his 1820 work focusing on the moral teachings of which figure?
questions.explanation: He wrote 'The Precepts of Jesus' (1820), emphasizing the moral teachings of Jesus.

questions.q: Raja Ram Mohan Roy travelled to England in 1830 partly as an envoy of which ruler?
questions.explanation: He went to England as an envoy of the Mughal Emperor Akbar II to press the emperor's case.

questions.q: In 1822 Raja Ram Mohan Roy founded which institution in Calcutta to teach English and Western subjects?
questions.explanation: Roy established the Anglo-Hindu School in Calcutta in 1822 to provide instruction in English and Western scientific subjects.

questions.q: Roy opposed government plans to fund which kind of institution, favouring scientific education instead?
questions.explanation: He famously opposed funding a purely traditional Sanskrit college, urging modern scientific education.

questions.q: Raja Ram Mohan Roy's first major theological work, written around 1803-1804 in Persian, was titled what?
questions.explanation: 'Tuhfat-ul-Muwahhidin' (A Gift to Monotheists) was among his earliest works, arguing for monotheism.

questions.q: Raja Ram Mohan Roy started which Bengali-language weekly newspaper in 1821?
questions.explanation: He founded 'Sambad Kaumudi,' a Bengali-language weekly, in 1821 as part of his pioneering reformist journalism.

questions.q: The Brahmo Sabha founded by Roy promoted worship of what?
questions.explanation: The Brahmo Sabha promoted monotheistic worship of one formless God and rejected idolatry.

questions.q: Roy's reform organization later evolved under Debendranath Tagore and Keshab Chandra Sen into what?
questions.explanation: The Brahmo Sabha became the Brahmo Samaj, carried forward by Debendranath Tagore and Keshab Chandra Sen.

questions.q: Raja Ram Mohan Roy is buried at Arnos Vale Cemetery in which English city?
questions.explanation: He died at Bristol in 1833 and is buried at Arnos Vale Cemetery there.

questions.q: Sati was legally abolished by Bengal Sati Regulation in which year?
questions.explanation: The Bengal Sati Regulation, abolishing sati, was enacted in 1829.

questions.q: In which year was Raja Ram Mohan Roy born?
questions.explanation: He was born in 1772, though some older sources give 1774.

questions.q: Roy translated and commented upon several Upanishads, drawing his monotheism chiefly from which body of texts?
questions.explanation: He grounded his monotheism in the Vedanta and the Upanishads, which he translated and expounded.

questions.q: Before his reform career Roy worked for a period with which colonial institution as a revenue official?
questions.explanation: He served for some years in the revenue administration of the East India Company before devoting himself to reform.

questions.q: Roy debated against missionaries from which famous Baptist mission base in Bengal over religious questions?
questions.explanation: He engaged in theological debate with the Serampore Baptist missionaries in Bengal.

questions.q: The Mughal Emperor who bestowed the title 'Raja' on Ram Mohan Roy was which one?
questions.explanation: Akbar II (also called Akbar Shah II) conferred the title 'Raja' on Ram Mohan Roy around 1830.

questions.q: Roy's campaign against sati was opposed by orthodox conservatives who formed a rival body called what?
questions.explanation: Conservative opponents formed the Dharma Sabha in 1830 to defend sati and resist Roy's reforms.

questions.q: Roy is also credited with pioneering work for press freedom, protesting restrictions on the Indian press under which official's regulations?
questions.explanation: Roy protested the press restrictions imposed by acting Governor-General John Adam in 1823.
